prompt: Create a cinematic vertical editorial poster portrait set inside an old dim library on a rainy, melancholic day. A young woman with long wet black hair stands in right profile beside tall wooden bookshelves, her face partly obscured by shadow and hair, wearing a fitted white sleeveless top, high-waisted dark trousers, and an oversized black coat slipped off one shoulder. She holds a lit cigarette near her face with one hand; visible smoke curls upward in soft blue-gray wisps. The environment is warm, dark, and atmospheric: blurred rows of old books on the right, wooden reading tables on the left, pendant lamps glowing in the background, shallow depth of field, soft film grain, muted brown and charcoal color palette, and a damp, nostalgic texture as if paused in an arthouse film. Compose her on the right half of the frame, cropped from mid-thigh upward, leaving negative space on the left for typography. Add exactly 3 text elements on the left: the large elegant serif headline stacked in three lines reading LOST IN THOUGHTS; a thin horizontal decorative divider line beneath it with small dots at both ends; and a small serif caption below reading Somewhere between silence and memory, I found pieces of me.. Use refined magazine-style English typography, off-white text, high contrast against the dark blurred background. Photorealistic, cinematic lighting, moody library ambience, realistic skin and wet hair, no plastic retouching, no extra people, no watermark, no additional text.
